Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The wild six weeks for NanoClaw's creator that led to a deal with Docker 18 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. The Mrs Fractal: Mirror, Rotate, Scale (2025) 35 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Okmain: How to pick an OK main colour of an image 203 upvotes, 40 comments
  4. Can I run AI locally? 480 upvotes, 129 comments
  5. Using Thunderbird for RSS 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. The Accidental Room (2018) 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Gvisor on Raspbian 54 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Lost Doctor Who Episodes Found 140 upvotes, 41 comments
  9. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We'll Be "Stunned" by NSA Under Section 702 208 upvotes, 72 comments
  10. Removing recursion via explicit callstack simulation 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it 51 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Meta Platforms: Lobbying, dark money, and the App Store Accountability Act 1077 upvotes, 463 comments
  13. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 268 upvotes, 106 comments
  14. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 196 upvotes, 90 comments
  15. E2E encrypted messaging on Instagram will no longer be supported after 8 May 308 upvotes, 165 comments
  16. Bucketsquatting is (finally) dead 278 upvotes, 149 comments
  17. TUI Studio – visual terminal UI design tool 446 upvotes, 251 comments
  18. Launch HN: Captain (YC W26) – Automated RAG for Files 34 upvotes, 13 comments
  19. Hammerspoon 45 upvotes, 19 comments
  20. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  21. Stanford researchers report first recording of a blue whale's heart rate (2019) 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 58 upvotes, 39 comments
  23. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 187 upvotes, 165 comments
  24. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 27 upvotes, 17 comments
  25. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 69 upvotes, 59 comments
  26. Your phone is an entire computer 121 upvotes, 115 comments
  27. Digg is gone again 12 upvotes, 7 comments
  28. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 84 upvotes, 103 comments
  29. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 95 upvotes, 134 comments
  30. Militaries are scrambling to create their own Starlink 41 upvotes, 61 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 14 March 2026. Source here.
